title: Live tab — WebSocket feed + polled straddle value (graceful, honest labeling)
acceptance_criteria: useLiveTicks opens a WebSocket to /ws/ticks using a runtime-built ws/wss URL from window.location (NOT a hardcoded host), and parses the RUNNING server shape: ignore {type:'connected'}, on {type:'tick'} read numeric `ltp`. It must NOT use the stale {fields.straddleValue} shape.
Connection status is tracked accurately as connecting | connected | disconnected and reflects real socket state.
Reconnect uses exponential backoff with jitter capped (~3s -> ~30s), and pauses while document is hidden (visibilitychange); it must not produce a reconnect storm against a dead server.
Effect is React 18 StrictMode-safe: cleanup closes the socket, clears timers, and detaches onclose before manual close so teardown does not arm a reconnect. No duplicate sockets/timers leak.
Tick buffer is bounded (cap ~300 points) so memory does not grow unbounded.

LiveView renders: a connection-status pill, the latest NIFTY index value (from ltp) with last-update time, and a small Lightweight Charts line of recent ticks LABELED as a synthetic/dev feed (never as a real straddle value).

LiveView polls GET /api/straddle/latest on a fixed interval (continuous polling, per user decision) via the T-04 api helper; while the endpoint returns {data:null} it shows an honest 'straddle feed not yet connected' notice, and will render the real value automatically once data appears.
Named exports only; TypeScript strict passes.
output_format: code plus plain English explanation of every non-obvious decision
